name: ferry-house
summary: A small riverbank cabin where the ferry operator lives and works. A dock, a landing ramp, a fee-table, a signal bell. The river is the real center of the room.

primary_palette.note: cabin plus dock — bell on a post, ropes coiled, boat at mooring
working dock — tackle and fish-racks, cabin off to one side
the raft itself at the waterline — hauling-rope across the river

story_prompts: ferryman present, tired, willing to haggle — standard fee posted, negotiable
ferry is gone — rope cut, cabin door open, signs of struggle
the ferryman is dead in the cabin — raft still at the dock, someone else ran it across first
ferryman is a smuggler — crates under the dock, double the usual goods
a charon figure — takes coin in silence, asks no questions, sees everything

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: outdoor riverside — bright daylight, lantern-post for dusk arrivals

anti_patterns: the cabin is a small prop-structure on a riverside lot, not a full building interior
do not center the cabin — push it to one side, leave the dock and shore dominant
the waterline is a hard edge — mark it clearly with dock-posts and a texture change
working gear belongs within arm's reach of the dock, not inside the cabin
